message:
fix(raptorq): add ESI multiset hash to decode proof for replay verification beyond truncated preview

ReceivedSummary now computes and stores an esi_multiset_hash — a
deterministic hash of the full (ESI, is_source) multiset — alongside
the existing truncated ESI preview list. When the number of received
symbols exceeds MAX_RECEIVED_SYMBOLS, the preview list is truncated to
keep proof sizes bounded, but this previously created a blind spot where
two different symbol sets could produce identical proofs as long as they
agreed on the first MAX_RECEIVED_SYMBOLS lowest ESIs.

The multiset hash covers all received symbols regardless of truncation,
so replay verification via compare_proofs now catches divergence in
high-ESI symbols that fall outside the preview window.

received_esi_multiset_hash() sorts the (ESI, is_source) pairs for
determinism and hashes them through the existing DetHasher.

Tests added:
- received_summary_hash_changes_when_high_esis_change_beyond_preview:
  constructs two symbol sets that agree on preview ESIs but differ in
  high ESIs, verifying the multiset hash distinguishes them
- replay_verification_rejects_high_esi_divergence: full end-to-end test
  that encodes an object, creates proofs from two different symbol sets
  (both decodable), and verifies that replay verification rejects the
  divergent proof

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
